ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Singapore economic rice (cai fan). Mapped to the closest parent class 'cooked rice dish' (FOODON_00002187) as an approximate anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Singapore cai fan. A00LC (rice-based mixed dish) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific mixed rice dish format.

allergens: gluten (possible from soy sauce or oyster sauce)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of economic rice with three vegetable dishes and one meat dish typically weighs approximately 400 g, as commonly served at Singapore hawker centres and coffee shops.

nutrient_source.title: Economic Rice (3 Vegetables, 1 Meat) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database for 'Economic Rice (3 Vegetables, 1 Meat)' (cr_id F05100048). All per-100g values are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, and micronutrients not reported in the HPB record are set to -1 (data not available).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice (steamed, Asian-style)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Singapore economic rice (cai fan) as a composite dish. GI of 72 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for steamed white rice (Asian-style), which is the dominant carbohydrate component of this dish. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 52.6) / 100 ≈ 38.1, based on a 400 g serving providing approximately 52.6 g carbohydrate (13.15 g/100g × 400 g). The vegetable and meat components are expected to modestly attenuate the effective GI in practice.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 233 mg/100g; estimated 932 mg per 400 g serving, approaching 40% of the 2,300 mg daily limit in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced sauce (soy sauce, oyster sauce) on vegetable and meat dishes to lower sodium intake, and choose steamed or braised lean protein over heavily seasoned preparations.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI white rice base (GI 72) with 13.15 g carbohydrate per 100 g and a high glycaemic load of approximately 38 per standard serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce the rice portion to half a scoop, increase the proportion of non-starchy vegetables, and choose a lean protein such as steamed chicken or tofu to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat at 5.34 g/100g with unknown saturated fat fraction; sodium at 233 mg/100g is a secondary concern for blood pressure management
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should select vegetable dishes prepared with minimal oil, choose lean white meat or fish over red meat or fatty pork, and request reduced sauce to limit sodium and saturated fat exposure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Variable meat selection may include red meat rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which can amplify inflammatory pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should choose chicken, fish, or tofu as the meat component and request less oil in vegetable stir-frying to reduce pro-inflammatory fatty acid intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Economic rice, known in Mandarin as 经济饭 (jīng jì fàn) or cai fan, is one of Singapore's most iconic and affordable hawker meals. It consists of a scoop of steamed white rice served on a plate alongside a self-selected combination of pre-cooked dishes displayed in large trays at the stall. The '3 vegetables, 1 meat' configuration — three vegetable side dishes and one meat dish — is the most common ordering pattern and represents a balanced, budget-friendly meal. Typical vegetable dishes include stir-fried leafy greens (kangkong, kai lan, cabbage), braised tofu, egg dishes, or lightly seasoned bean sprouts. The meat component varies widely and may include braised pork belly, steamed chicken, fried fish, or char siu. The rice is soft and slightly sticky, the vegetables are lightly seasoned with soy sauce or oyster sauce, and the meat is typically savoury and umami-rich. Per 100 g, the dish provides 121 kcal, 4.89 g protein, 5.34 g fat, and 13.15 g carbohydrate, with 1.47 g dietary fibre and 233 mg sodium. The high-GI white rice base (GI ≈ 72) creates a high glycaemic load per standard serving (~38), making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate-to-high due to sauces used in cooking, which is a concern for hypertension management. The dish is widely consumed across all ethnic communities in Singapore for weekday lunches and is available at virtually every hawker centre and coffee shop (kopitiam) island-wide.
common_user_queries: How many calories in economic rice 3 veg 1 meat?
Is economic rice suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium in economic rice?
Is economic rice good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05100048) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The HPB is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using steamed white rice as the analogous food, which is the dominant carbohydrate source in this dish. Confidence is rated high because the HPB record is an exact match for this dish.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Economic Rice (3 Vegetables, 1 Meat) (F05100048) as of April 2026. Detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data are not available in the HPB record for this composite dish. The input record incorrectly included 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' dietary tags; these have been removed as the dish contains meat. The halal flag is set to false because the meat component is variable and may include pork. GI is an analogous estimate based on steamed white rice (GI 72) from the University of Sydney GI Database; the actual composite GI of the dish may be modestly lower due to the protein, fat, and fibre contributions of the vegetable and meat components.
